news.title: STAT+: OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma set to dissolve after judge approves its criminal sentence
news.summary: At the hearing, the judge heard from mothers who lost sons to overdose, a teenager born into withdrawal, and people who spent years dealing with addiction treatment.

news.title: Remembering J. Craig Venter: a relentless scientist who changed biotech — and was all too easily misunderstood
news.summary: J. Craig Venter, the scientist whose relentless ambition helped turn genetics from an artisanal trade into an industrialized information machine, died at 79.

news.title: STAT+: Katherine Szarama named acting director of FDA’s vaccines and biologics center
news.summary: Katherine Szarama, who joined the FDA last year to serve as Vinay Prasad’s deputy, will now fill his shoes at CBER.

news.title: STAT+: In her own words: Surgeon general nominee Nicole Saphier expresses enthusiasm and caution for MAHA
news.summary: Examining the past statements and writings of Trump's third surgeon general nominee, Dr. Nicole Saphier, on vaccines, autism, peptides, diet and more.

news.title: STAT+: Hair-raising trial results, and Servier’s M&A wishlist
news.summary: Why are investors excited about hair loss drugs? Will AI make clinical trials run more smoothly? And more in "The Readout LOUD," STAT's weekly biotech podcast.

news.title: STAT+: As artificial intelligence shows off diagnostic chops, scientists reckon with the way forward
news.summary: On Thursday, researchers published in Science the results of a study that tested an OpenAI model on diagnostic and clinical reasoning tasks.

news.title: Trump drops Casey Means as surgeon general nominee, opts for radiologist Nicole Saphier
news.summary: Trump slammed Sen. Bill Cassidy for blocking his surgeon general pick, as he nominated a replacement.

news.title: STAT+: FDA wants to exclude weight loss drugs from a compounding list
news.summary: FDA moves to remove semaglutide, tirzepatide, and liraglutide from its 503B compounding list, saying there's no shortage of GLP-1 drugs from Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly.

news.title: STAT+: Five-way obesity drug is super effective — in mice
news.summary: A 'quintuple agonist,' Alector halts it Alzheimer's trial, and more biotech news from The Readout

news.title: STAT+: DOJ, Elevance spar over access to top exec in Medicare Advantage fraud case
news.summary: Elevance said a top exec would depart, days after the Justice Department asked to depose him.

news.title: STAT+: Hinge has a new FDA-cleared migraine device
news.summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: FDA's plan to use AI to speed up trials, Hinge's new migraine device, and boosting cancer screening with texts.

news.title: STAT+: Cigna will exit ACA individual markets next year, adding to tumult for patients
news.summary: ACA membership is tumbling after Congress failed to extend extra subsidies that helped people afford the coverage.

news.title: STAT+: Democrats go hard on health care affordability
news.summary: The Senate's top Democrat unveiled a health policy outline that calls for lowering patients' costs across the board.

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a Supreme Court hearing on ‘skinny labels,’ AstraZeneca U.K. expansion, and more
news.summary: AstraZeneca will resume expansion of its U.K. R&D operations after the trade deal with the U.S. included measures to lift U.K. drug spending

news.title: Smog from wildfires increasingly harmful, data show
news.summary: American baby formula testing, Medicaid work requirements, and more Morning Rounds health news

news.title: Education Department finalizes industry-opposed student loan caps
news.summary: The final rule excludes post-baccalaureate health roles like advanced practice registered nurses and physician associates from higher borrowing caps permitted for "professional" degrees, like physicians. Healthcare groups had warned it could hamstring the clinical workforce pipeline.

news.title: Tenet Healthcare met Q1's volume curveballs with 'old-fashioned discipline'
news.summary: With much of the quarter's volume disruptions sequestered to January and early February, CEO Saum Sutaria, M.D., said Tenet was able to regain ground by adjusting its expenses to better reflect demand, rebooking ambulatory procedures and “focusing more on some of our other type of work in the hospitals.”

news.title: Trump pulls surgeon general nomination of Casey Means, names Nicole Saphier as new pick
news.summary: The president blamed Senate HELP Chair Bill Cassidy for blocking the confirmation of Means, an influential voice within the Make America Healthy Again movement. His new choice is a practicing radiologist, Fox News medical contributor and an author previously critical of the government's COVID-19 pandemic response.

news.title: DOJ launches West Coast Health Care Strike Force to target fraud in Arizona, Nevada, Northern California
news.summary: The Department of Justice has formed a new fraud strike force to target schemes across Arizona, Nevada and Northern California, the agency announced Thursday.

news.title: Cigna to exit ACA market, pursue strategic alternatives for eviCore unit
news.summary: The Cigna Group beat the Street on both earnings and revenue in the first quarter of 2026, posting $1.65 billion in profit for Q1.

news.title: Fierce Healthcare Fundraising Tracker '26: Gravity Rail tackles AI-powered patient engagement; Zócalo Health pockets $15M
news.summary: Keep up with all the venture capital dollars being funneled into the health tech and digital health industries.

news.title: Functional medicine provider Parsley Health now in-network nationwide
news.summary: Today’s nationwide expansion represents a tenfold increase in coverage, per the company. The company offers healthcare via telehealth nationwide or in-person in Los Angeles and New York City.

news.title: Waystar kicks off 2026 with strong growth as it targets AI at $100B RCM labor pool
news.summary: Waystar is pivoting its focus from task-level automation toward agentic workflows. "That shift unlocks a much larger opportunity, the approximately $100 billion in annual revenue cycle labor services performed across the industry today," CEO Matt Hawkins said.

news.title: Viz.ai partners with National Rural Health Association to expand AI understanding, access to rural hospitals
news.summary: The newly announced collaboration between the two organizations will provide rural health systems with practical guidance on AI implementation through educational opportunities, case studies and more.

news.title: Photon, maker of modern prescription infrastructure, nabs $16M to scale
news.summary: The lack of visibility into price and convenience in today's prescribing ecosystem leads to unnecessary transfers, unanswered phone calls and abandoned fills, Photon executives say.

news.title: Aidoc banks $150M backed by Goldman Sachs to scale clinical AI foundation model
news.summary: Clinical artificial intelligence startup Aidoc landed a $150 million funding round, less than a year after raising the same nine-figure amount in new financing.

news.title: Teladoc Health reports strong momentum behind BetterHelp insurance shift, CEO says
news.summary: Teladoc Health reported a 2% decline in revenue in the first quarter, but the telehealth giant touted "meaningful progress" in scaling insurance coverage for its BetterHelp mental health business as a catalyst for future growth.

news.title: AstraZeneca's camizestrant ambitions stumble as FDA panel rejects novel oral SERD proposal
news.summary: An FDA expert panel on Thursday voted 6 to 3 against AstraZeneca's proposal of using its oral SERD camizestrant in a novel first-line switch setting in HR-positive, HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer, dealing an early blow to the British pharma's $5 billion peak sales plan.

news.title: Fierce Biotech Layoff Tracker 2026: Autolus decreases headcount by 13%; Replimune reduces staff
news.summary: As always, if you know of layoffs occurring at a biotech, please reach out to the Fierce Biotech editorial team.

news.title: BMS calls time on Zymeworks collab, ending work on phase 1 cancer bispecific
news.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb has called time on a bispecific antibody partnership with Zymeworks that the Big Pharma inherited from Celgene.

news.title: Indivior warns of ‘significant impact’ on R&D team after opioid use disorder drug fails phase 2
news.summary: Indivior ended internal development of an opioid use disorder candidate after a phase 2 trial missed its primary endpoint.

news.title: Leo Pharma pays $50M for Replay and its preclinical skin disease gene therapy
news.summary: Danish dermatology specialist Leo Pharma is paying $50 million for U.S. gene writing biotech Replay and its preclinical gene therapy for a rare skin disease.

news.title: Labcorp raises full-year guidance, boosted by diagnostics, lab services
news.summary: Labcorp is raising its 2026 guidance, buoyed by demand for its diagnostics and central laboratory businesses.

news.title: BeOne signs $2B deal for option on preclinical trispecific antibody from China’s Huahui
news.summary: BeOne Medicines is paying $20 million upfront to reserve the right to buy a preclinical PD-1xCTLA-4xVEGF trispecific antibody from China’s Huahui Health.

news.title: GSK’s $700M Alector bet blows up as Alzheimer’s drug flunks phase 2
news.summary: GSK and Alector have stopped a phase 2 trial of their Alzheimer’s disease drug candidate after an interim analysis found the study was unlikely to hit its primary endpoint.

news.title: Avalyn heads to Nasdaq with oversized $300M IPO to fund reformulated respiratory drugs
news.summary: Avalyn Pharma has overshot its expectations with a $300 million IPO that the biotech will use to fund late-stage studies of its inhaled versions of approved respiratory drugs.

news.title: CareDx expands into cancer testing with $260M Naveris buy
news.summary: Transplant-focused precision medicine company CareDx is making a move into the oncology space, scooping up Massachusetts-based biotech Naveris in a deal worth up to $260 million.

news.title: AstraZeneca plucks preclinical EGFR degrader from Pinetree for $25M
news.summary: AstraZeneca has paid $25 million to pick up a preclinical EGFR degrader that was at the center of its existing pact with Pinetree Therapeutics.

news.title: FDA hits Newron’s phase 3 schizophrenia trial with clinical hold in US after patient death
news.summary: The FDA has halted U.S. enrollment for Newron Pharmaceuticals’ phase 3 schizophrenia trial, following the death of a patient outside of the country.

news.title: 蚂蚁阿福，开辟AI新战场
news.summary: 蚂蚁阿福的“行为经济学”

papers.title: ClawGym: A Scalable Framework for Building Effective Claw Agents
papers.summary: Claw-style environments support multi-step workflows over local files, tools, and persistent workspace states. However, scalable development around these environments remains constrained by the absence of a systematic framework, especially one for synthesizing verifiable training data and integrating it with agent training and diagnostic evaluation. To address this challenge, we present ClawGym, a scalable framework that supports the full lifecycle of Claw-style personal agent development. Concretely, we construct ClawGym-SynData, a diverse dataset of 13.5K filtered tasks synthesized from pers

papers.title: DV-World: Benchmarking Data Visualization Agents in Real-World Scenarios
papers.summary: Real-world data visualization (DV) requires native environmental grounding, cross-platform evolution, and proactive intent alignment. Yet, existing benchmarks often suffer from code-sandbox confinement, single-language creation-only tasks, and assumption of perfect intent. To bridge these gaps, we introduce DV-World, a benchmark of 260 tasks designed to evaluate DV agents across real-world professional lifecycles. DV-World spans three domains: DV-Sheet for native spreadsheet manipulation including chart and dashboard creation as well as diagnostic repair; DV-Evolution for adapting and restruct
